reasoning: Reject — completely wrong domain. This candidate is a transport and fleet operations professional with 10+ years in EV mobility, ambulance fleet management, and driver/vendor coordination. There is zero evidence of Python development, any programming language, back-end frameworks, databases, or software engineering of any kind. The recruiter specifically wants someone with Python projects and AI model training experience — this candidate has none. With 0 of 15 must-have technical skills met, this resume should not advance under any circumstances, regardless of how thin the hiring pool is.
strengths: OPERATIONS SCALE: Manages 51 electric buses across 76 live schedules and previously oversaw fuel operations for 1,000+ emergency vehicles — demonstrates strong execution at scale, but this is fleet ops, not software engineering.

DOMAIN DEPTH: 10+ years of progressive experience across EV mobility, FMCG fleet, and emergency services with hands-on GPS/telematics and MIS reporting — highly specialised in transport, entirely irrelevant to Python development.

COMPLIANCE & COORDINATION: Proven vendor management, RTO regulatory compliance, and real-time control room operations — useful in operations roles but contributes nothing to a software developer evaluation.
gaps: ZERO TECHNICAL SKILLS: Not a single Python, Django, Flask, FastAPI, SQL, Git, Docker, or any software development skill is present anywhere in the resume. This candidate cannot write code, and interviewing them for a Python Developer role would be a waste of everyone's time.

NO AI/ML EXPOSURE WHATSOEVER: The recruiter explicitly requires Python project experience and AI model training. This candidate has no educational or professional background in computer science, data science, or machine learning — this is a complete and total mismatch on the key differentiator.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated; candidate appears currently employed but this is irrelevant given domain mismatch.
JD requires In-office in Bangalore; candidate is based in Hyderabad.
JD requires 0 years relevant experience so no experience gap penalty applies, but candidate has 0 Python experience regardless.
JD gender preference is Any; no penalty applied.
No college preference or exclusion specified in JD; no adjustment applied.

availability_reason: Current role at GreenCell Mobility is listed as Apr 2025 to Present with no notice period stated.
alternative_role_fit: Fleet Operations Manager / Transport Operations Lead
rare_assets: 10+ years of EV fleet operations experience including electric bus network management — rare in the transport domain.
Fuel and fleet management for 1,000+ emergency ambulance vehicles across a state-level government health service.
self_reported_unverified: Claims real-time monitoring and operational bridge responsibilities across multiple depots — scope and individual contribution versus team effort unverifiable.
'Earlier Experience (2013-2021)' is summarised in one line with no employer names, roles, or verifiable details.
interview_flags: Do not interview — candidate is in the wrong domain entirely and should be redirected to transport/logistics/operations roles.
If contacted in error, ask: 'Do you have any background in software development, Python programming, or AI/ML projects?' — expected answer: No.
Clarify if candidate applied intentionally or if there was a resume submission error.
Probe the 2013-2021 gap narrative — eight years compressed into one line with no employer names raises resume integrity concerns.
Ask about any technical tools used in fleet operations (GPS platforms, MIS systems) to determine if there is any adjacent tech exposure worth noting for future ops-tech hybrid roles.
